summary: Rent vs buy a home: break-even horizon against cash-safety and affordability constraints.

cases.summary: Do not treat this as a buy/rent preference yet; a hard cash-safety constraint is failing.
cases.focus: Your post-purchase emergency fund drops below the safe floor.
cases.deprioritize: Do not optimize appreciation or rent assumptions until the down payment no longer wipes out your safety buffer.
cases.next_step: Rebuild the post-purchase emergency fund, lower the price, or shrink the down payment before revisiting the buy case.

cases.summary: Do not treat this as a buy/rent preference yet; the monthly housing cost is above a safe share of income.
cases.focus: Monthly housing cost is too large a share of after-tax income.
cases.deprioritize: Do not fine-tune appreciation or break-even assumptions while affordability itself is failing.
cases.next_step: Get the all-in monthly housing cost down to a safe income share before comparing buy vs rent.

next_step_when_numeric.template: Bring the home price to about {max_home_price_within_budget} or below (at this rate and down payment) to clear the affordability ceiling, or change the income assumptions.

cases.summary: Lean toward renting. The hard constraints are fine, but you likely will not stay long enough for buying to beat renting.
cases.focus: The decision turns on how long you will stay, not on small cost tweaks.

focus_when_numeric.template: You expect to stay about {expected_years_in_home} years, but buying only breaks even after about {break_even_years} years on these assumptions.

cases.deprioritize: Emergency-fund safety and affordability already pass; do not keep re-estimating them. Small changes to tax or maintenance will not close a multi-year horizon gap.
cases.next_step: Pressure-test how confident you are in your length-of-stay; if it could realistically reach the break-even horizon, buying becomes reasonable.

cases.summary: Do not force a conclusion yet. The model is mostly waiting on one decision-defining number: how long you will stay.
cases.focus: The buy/rent answer hinges on your expected length of stay versus the break-even horizon.

focus_when_numeric.template: Buying breaks even after about {break_even_years} years here; the missing piece is how long you actually expect to stay.

cases.deprioritize: Do not spread effort across every rate assumption; the length-of-stay estimate dominates.
cases.next_step: Estimate, even as a range, how many years you realistically expect to keep this home.

default.summary: The current conclusion is conditional; the next useful step is to confirm length of stay and the few numbers that move the break-even horizon.
default.focus: Length of stay versus the break-even horizon is the dominant lever in this decision.
default.deprioritize: Do not spread effort evenly across every estimate; start with length of stay, price, rate and rent.
default.next_step: Confirm your expected length of stay and the comparable rent before tuning smaller assumptions.

rules.question: How many years do you realistically expect to keep and live in this home? A range is fine.
rules.why_template: Buying only beats renting once you pass the break-even horizon of about {break_even_text}, so length of stay is the decision-defining input.
rules.possible_conclusion_impact: Could move the conclusion between lean_no (rent) and lean_yes/recommend (buy).

rules.question: What mortgage rate can you actually lock right now for this purchase?
rules.why_this_question: The rate drives the monthly payment, affordability, and the break-even horizon all at once.
rules.possible_conclusion_impact: Could shift affordability between pass and fail and move the break-even horizon by years.

rules.question: What does it actually cost to rent an equivalent home in the same area today?
rules.why_this_question: The break-even horizon is a direct comparison against this rent; a wrong rent moves the whole conclusion.
rules.possible_conclusion_impact: Could flip the break-even comparison if the true comparable rent is materially different.

rules.question: What after-tax monthly income should this decision use right now?
rules.why_this_question: Affordability is judged as a share of after-tax income, and it is currently unresolved or failing.
rules.possible_conclusion_impact: Could move affordability from open or fail to pass, or confirm a hard affordability block.

rules.question: After the down payment and closing costs, how many months of expenses would your emergency fund still cover?
rules.why_this_question: Cash safety is a hard constraint, and a large down payment can quietly drop the buffer below a safe floor.
rules.possible_conclusion_impact: Could turn a hard cash-safety failure into a pass or confirm that buying is currently unsafe.
